definition: Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia fertility rates may decline faster than UN median projections, driven by smartphone-penetration mechanisms enabling faster diffusion of female education and aspirations, potentially collapsing the 'next billion consumers' thesis.
evidence: Bangladesh TFR fell from 6.6 (1980) to 2.0 (2023) faster than almost any precedent
India crossed below replacement in 2021 ahead of projections
open_questions: Whether Sub-Saharan Africa follows South Asian patterns
Investment implications for EM equity, commodities, sovereign debt
